BQBYTE TECHNOLOGIES
Supplier Governance & Compliance Policy
Version 3.2 | Effective Date: 01 September 2024
CONFIDENTIAL — FOR INTERNAL PROCUREMENT USE ONLY

Compliance Score below 75.
5.2 Escalation Triggers
Any supplier flagged High Risk for two consecutive quarters must be escalated to the Chief Procurement Officer
(CPO)  for  strategic  review.  The  CPO  review  determines  one  of  three  outcomes:  (a)  Supplier  Remediation  Plan,
(b) Parallel Sourcing Activation (see Section 10), or (c) Supplier Exit Programme initiation with 90-day wind-down
period.
5.3 Concentration Risk Rule
No single region (APAC, EMEA, LATAM, NA) may account for more than 45% of total annual procurement spend.
No single country may represent more than 25% of spend. If concentration limits are breached, the Procurement
team must submit a Diversification Plan within 60 days.
6. Sustainability & Compliance Requirements
6.1 Sustainability Score

automotive-grade components.
Raw Materials (RM): ISO 9001 + REACH compliance mandatory for all materials entering EU markets. ISO 14001
required for Tier-1 Raw Material suppliers.
Packaging  Materials  (PM):  ISO  9001  +  FSC  certification  mandatory  for  paper-based  packaging.  ISO  14001
required for Tier-1 PM suppliers.
Mechanical Components (MC): ISO 9001 mandatory. IATF 16949 required for any automotive supply chain use.
Specialty  Alloys  (SA):  ISO  9001  mandatory.  NADCAP  required  for  aerospace  applications.  AS9100  required  for
Tier-1 SA suppliers.
Industrial Textiles (IT): ISO 9001 + OEKO-TEX Standard 100 mandatory.
7. Audit Cadence & Scoring Methodology
7.1 Audit Frequency
Tier-1 suppliers are audited annually. Tier-2 suppliers are audited every six months. Tier-3 suppliers are audited
quarterly. Suppliers with an Active Disruption flag receive an additional unannounced spot audit within 30 days of
the flag being raised.

9. Disruption Response Procedures
When  a  supplier  Active  Disruption  is  flagged  (non-None  value  in  the  Active_Disruptions  field),  the  following
response tiers apply:
Level  1  —  Monitor  (Low  Risk  suppliers  with  disruption  flag):  Weekly  status  update  required  from  supplier.
BQBYTE Procurement team adjusts safety stock for affected SKU by +15%.
Level 2 — Manage (Medium Risk suppliers with disruption flag): Bi-weekly escalation calls. Safety stock adjusted
by +30%. Alternate supplier placed on 48-hour readiness notice.
Level 3 — Activate (High Risk suppliers with any disruption flag, or any supplier with two simultaneous disruption
flags): Immediate escalation to CPO. Alternate supplier activated for minimum 40% of volume within 10 business
days. Safety stock adjusted by +50%. Full RCA required within 15 business days.
Disruption  categories  requiring  mandatory  Level  3  response  regardless  of  risk  level:  export  control  restrictions,

Table of Contents
1. Purpose & Scope
2. Supplier Tier Classification Framework
3. Service Level Agreements (SLA) by Tier
4. Penalty & Incentive Structure
5. Risk Assessment & Escalation Protocol
6. Sustainability & Compliance Requirements
7. Audit Cadence & Scoring Methodology
8. Approved Certification Matrix
9. Disruption Response Procedures
10. Alternate Supplier Activation Rules

The Sustainability Score (0-100) assesses carbon footprint reporting, use of renewable energy, waste reduction
programmes, and adherence to ILO labour standards. Minimum required scores by tier:
Tier-1: Sustainability Score must be 80 or above. Tier-2: Score must be 60 or above. Tier-3: Score must be 45 or
above. Suppliers below their tier minimum are required to submit a Sustainability Improvement Plan (SIP) within
45 days of audit notification.
6.2 Mandatory Certification Requirements
All  suppliers  must  hold  ISO  9001  certification,  which  is  non-negotiable  regardless  of  tier  or  category.  Additional
certifications are required based on product category:
Electronic  Components  (EC):  ISO  9001  +  RoHS  compliance  mandatory.  AEC-Q100  strongly  recommended  for
automotive-grade components.
Raw Materials (RM): ISO 9001 + REACH compliance mandatory for all materials entering EU markets. ISO 14001
required for Tier-1 Raw Material suppliers.

quarterly. Suppliers with an Active Disruption flag receive an additional unannounced spot audit within 30 days of
the flag being raised.
Audit overdue threshold: Any supplier whose Last Audit Date is more than 14 months ago (for Tier-1), more than 7
months  ago  (for  Tier-2),  or  more  than  4  months  ago  (for  Tier-3)  is  classified  as  Audit  Overdue  and  placed  on
provisional SWL status.
7.2 Audit Score Calculation
The  Compliance  Score  is  calculated  as  a  weighted  composite:  Quality  Systems  (30%),  Document  Compliance
(20%),  Corrective  Action  Closure  (20%),  Regulatory  Adherence  (15%),  and  Ethical  Standards  (15%).  Scores
below 70 at a scheduled audit trigger a mandatory follow-up audit within 60 days at the supplier's cost.

Disruption  categories  requiring  mandatory  Level  3  response  regardless  of  risk  level:  export  control  restrictions,
active labour strikes, regulatory enforcement actions, and port closure events exceeding 72 hours.
10. Alternate Supplier Activation Rules
Every Tier-2 and Tier-3 supplier must have a designated Alternate Supplier (Alt_Supplier_ID). Tier-1 suppliers are
strongly recommended to maintain an alternate. Alternate suppliers must:
(a) Be capable of supplying the same product SKU within 120% of the primary supplier's quoted lead time.
(b) Hold all mandatory certifications for the relevant product category (Section 6.2 and Section 8).
(c) Not share the same geopolitical risk region as the primary supplier in a Level 3 activation scenario.
(d) Not be on SWL status at the time of activation.
Alternate  supplier  activation  does  not  automatically  void  the  primary  supplier's  contract.  Activation  volume

the  authoritative  source  for  all  quantitative  supplier  metrics  including  On-Time  Delivery  Rate  (OTD_Rate_Pct),
Defect Rate, Compliance Score, and Sustainability Score.
2. Supplier Tier Classification Framework
BQBYTE  classifies  all  suppliers  into  three  strategic  tiers  based  on  annual  spend,  criticality,  and  performance
history.  Tier  assignment  determines  applicable  SLAs,  audit  frequency,  payment  terms  eligibility,  and  risk
management obligations.

to Tier-3 triggers immediate mandatory corrective action plan (CAP).
3. Service Level Agreements (SLA) by Tier
The following SLAs are contractually binding. Failure to meet SLA thresholds beyond the grace periods specified
activates penalty clauses defined in Section 4.

1. Purpose & Scope
This policy governs all procurement relationships within the BQBYTE Technologies global supply chain network. It
establishes   binding   performance   standards,   risk   thresholds,   compliance   requirements,   and   operational
procedures  applicable  to  all  25  active  Tier-1,  Tier-2,  and  Tier-3  designated  suppliers  across  the  APAC,  EMEA,
LATAM, and NA regions.
All  suppliers  are  identified  by  a  unique  Supplier  ID  (format:  SUP-XXX)  and  assigned  a  Product  SKU  (format:
SKU-[CATEGORY]-[NNNN]).  Category  codes  are:  EC  (Electronic  Components),  RM  (Raw  Materials),  MC
(Mechanical Components), PM (Packaging Materials), IT (Industrial Textiles), SA (Specialty Alloys).
This document must be read in conjunction with the Supplier Performance Data register (CSV format), which is
the  authoritative  source  for  all  quantitative  supplier  metrics  including  On-Time  Delivery  Rate  (OTD_Rate_Pct),

PO issuance to 20% of prior quarter volume.
4. Penalty & Incentive Structure
4.1 OTD Penalty Tiers
Penalties  are  assessed  quarterly  based  on  rolling  OTD  performance.  Penalty  credits  are  applied  as  invoice
deductions on the following quarter's payments.
OTD Performance BandApplicable TiersPenalty Rate (% of quarterly invoice)
Below 70.0%All Tiers8.0% — and mandatory CAP within 10 days
70.0% – 74.9%Tier-1 only5.0%
75.0% – 79.9%Tier-1 and Tier-23.5%
80.0% – 83.9%Tier-1 only2.0%
84.0% – 86.9%Tier-1 only1.0%
87.0% – 92.9%No penalty (Tier-2/3 acceptable)0%
93.0% and aboveAll Tiers — target0% (Volume Rebate eligible for Tier-1)

4.2 Volume Rebate Program (Tier-1 Only)
Tier-1 suppliers with OTD at or above 93.0%, Defect Rate below 0.5%, and Sustainability Score at or above 85 for
a full calendar year qualify for an annual volume rebate of 2.5% of total annual invoice value. Rebate eligibility is
assessed in January of the following year.
4.3 Defect Penalty Escalation
Defect rates exceeding the tier maximum for two consecutive quarters result in a penalty surcharge of 4.0% of the
affected  quarter's  invoice  in  addition  to  any  OTD  penalties.  If  the  defect  rate  is  above  5.0%  for  any  single
shipment, a Quality Containment Fee of USD 15,000 is assessed per occurrence.
5. Risk Assessment & Escalation Protocol
5.1 Risk Level Definitions
Each  supplier  is  assigned  a  Risk  Level  (Low,  Medium,  High)  assessed  quarterly  by  the  BQBYTE  Procurement
Risk  Committee.  Risk  level  is  determined  by  geopolitical  exposure,  single-source  dependency,  financial  health,

3.1 On-Time Delivery (OTD)
OTD is measured as the percentage of purchase orders delivered within the confirmed lead time window, with a
tolerance  of  +/-  2  business  days.  Tier-1  suppliers  must  maintain  OTD  at  or  above  93.0%.  Tier-2  suppliers  must
maintain OTD at or above 84.0%. Tier-3 suppliers must maintain OTD at or above 75.0%. OTD is calculated on a
rolling 90-day basis.
3.2 Defect Rate
Defect rate is expressed as a percentage of units rejected at incoming quality inspection (IQI). Tier-1 maximum
permissible defect rate is 0.99%. Tier-2 maximum is 2.50%. Tier-3 maximum is 4.00%. Any single shipment with a
defect  rate  exceeding  8.0%  (regardless  of  tier)  triggers  an  immediate  hold  and  mandatory  root-cause  analysis
(RCA) submission within 5 business days.

Low Risk: No active disruption flags, Compliance Score above 85, no geopolitical exposure, diversified logistics

Medium  Risk:  One  or  more  of  the  following  —  geopolitical  tension  flag,  seasonal  disruption  risk  (e.g.  monsoon,

High  Risk:  Two  or  more  Medium  risk  factors  present,  OR  any  of  —  active  labour  dispute,  export  control
restrictions, currency volatility exceeding 15% YTD, overdue audit (more than 12 months since last audit date), or
Compliance Score below 75.

3.3 Lead Time Compliance
Quoted  lead  times  must  be  honoured  within  the  agreed  tolerance.  Lead  times  exceeding  50  calendar  days  are
subject  to  additional  review  under  the  Extended  Lead  Time  Review  Protocol  (ELTRP),  regardless  of  tier.
Currently,  suppliers  SUP-005  (42  days),  SUP-012  (38  days),  SUP-015  (45  days),  and  SUP-023  (36  days)  carry
lead times approaching or at ELTRP review boundaries.
3.4 Compliance Score Floor
The  Compliance  Score  (0-100)  is  a  composite  metric  incorporating  regulatory  adherence,  documentation
completeness, audit findings, and corrective action closure rates. Any supplier with a Compliance Score below 60
at any audit is placed on Supplier Watch List (SWL) status, regardless of tier designation. SWL status limits new
PO issuance to 20% of prior quarter volume.
